zh.Echingham, Sussex, England: 英國英格蘭素可艾欽加姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan FitzAlan於大約1360年出生在英國素可阿盧德爾時，她的父親Sir John FitzAlan11歲，母親Eleanor Mautravers16歲。她於1401年與Sir William II Echyngham在英國英格蘭素可艾欽加姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1404年9月1日在英國素可艾欽加姆享年45歲，埋葬在英國素可艾欽加姆。

zh-hans.Echingham, Sussex, England: 英国英格兰素可艾欽加姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan FitzAlan于大约1360年出生在英国素可阿盧德爾时，她的父亲Sir John FitzAlan11岁，母亲Eleanor Mautravers16岁。她于1401年与Sir William II Echyngham在英国英格兰素可艾欽加姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1404年9月1日在英国素可艾欽加姆享年45岁，埋葬在英国素可艾欽加姆。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan FitzAlan was born about 1360, in Arundel, Sussex, England, her father, Sir John FitzAlan, was 11 and her mother, Eleanor Mautravers, was 16. She married Sir William II Echyngham in 1401, in Etchingham, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 1 September 1404, in Etchingham, Sussex, England, at the age of 45, and was buried in Etchingham, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan FitzAlan est née environ 1360, à Arundel, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Sir John FitzAlan, avait 11 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Mautravers, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William II Echyngham en 1401, à Etchingham, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 1 septembre 1404, à Etchingham, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterrée à Etchingham, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan FitzAlan um 1360 in Arundel, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John FitzAlan 11 und ihre Mutter Eleanor Mautravers 16. Sie hat 1401 in Etchingham, Sussex, England, United Kingdom Sir William II Echyngham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 1. September 1404 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Etchingham, Sussex, England und wurde in Etchingham, Sussex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan FitzAlan è nata il circa 1360 nel Arundel, Sussex, England, sua padre, Sir John FitzAlan, aveva 11 anni e sua madre, Eleanor Mautravers, aveva 16 anni. Lei sposato Sir William II Echyngham nel 1401, nel Etchingham, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 1 settembre 1404, nel Etchingham, Sussex, England, all'età di 45 anni, e fu sepolta a Etchingham, Sussex, England.

ja.Arundel, Sussex, England: イギリススセックスアルンデル
ja.Etchingham, Sussex, England: イギリススセックスエトチンガム
ja.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlanが約1360年にイギリススセックスアルンデルで生まれた時，父Sir John FitzAlanは11歳，母Eleanor Mautraversは16歳であった。1401年にイギリスイギリススセックスエトチンガムでSir William II Echynghamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1404年9月1日にイギリススセックスエトチンガムで45歳で死去し，イギリススセックスエトチンガムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan FitzAlan(이)가 대략 1360년 영국 수세스 아런들에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John FitzAlan(은)는 11세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor Mautravers(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1401년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 트친그함에서 Sir William II Echyngham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1404년 9월 1일 영국 수세스 트친그함에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 트친그함에 묻혔다.

mn.Echingham, Sussex, England: Etchingham, Sūþsēaxe, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan FitzAlan nasceu aproximadamente 1360, em Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John FitzAlan, tinha 11 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor Mautravers, tinha 16 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William II Echyngham em 1401, em Etchingham, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1 de setembro de 1404, em Etchingham, Sussex, Inglaterra, com 45 anos, e foi sepultada em Etchingham, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan FitzAlan родилась около 1360 г. в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John FitzAlan было 11 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Mautravers, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William II Echyngham в 1401 г. в местности эчингхам, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 1 сентября 1404 г. в местности эчингхам, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 45 лет и была похоронена в местности эчингхам, суссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan FitzAlan nació aproximadamente 1360, en Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John FitzAlan, tenía 11 años y su mamá, Eleanor Mautravers, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Sir William II Echyngham en 1401, en Etchingham, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 1 de septiembre de 1404, en Etchingham, Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 45 años, y fue sepultada en Etchingham, Sussex, Inglaterra.
